cases.description: Existence probe only: TODAY() is non-deterministic (changes daily), so there is no fixed 'expected' value
cases.expected_note: Non-deterministic by design; harness/human should only check the engine recognizes the function (not #NAME?/#VALUE!) and returns a plausible large positive integer serial

cases.description: Wrapping TODAY() in YEAR() gives a coarser but still non-deterministic probe
cases.expected_note: Non-deterministic; harness/human should only check the result is a plausible 4-digit year (>= 2026 as of this test's authoring)

cases.description: Even though TODAY() itself is volatile, two calls within the SAME recalculation pass must return the identical value, so their difference is always exactly 0 -- a genuinely deterministic assertion about a volatile function
cases.expected_note: Both TODAY() calls are evaluated as part of one recalculation of one cell, so they cannot differ
